behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over text artifacts (template vs forbidden phase-instruction snippets). MOCK-MISS: this behavior is primarily parsing/matching logic, not a multi-module handoff failure. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess dependence is required. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration/System were considered, but behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies needing higher-level runtime infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the lint function template strings containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md and verify rejection with precise diagnostics; feed clean templates and verify pass.

behaviors.description: Fully assembled prompt from the real harness (after interpolation/fixtures) contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries: template assembly + fixture interpolation + final prompt composer. Unit tests on template fragments can miss leakage introduced during assembly. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess required, so not System. LLM-DEP: no model-quality dependence, so not Agentic. MULTI-STEP: no agent pipeline. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is about "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness" (local composition), not environment-dependent runtime effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-building pipeline with representative fixtures and assert the final composed prompt has zero matches against a leakage signature set derived from phase-specific SKILL.md instructions.

behaviors.description: Test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context, not hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is a wiring/provenance failure mode across loader + harness + test configuration. MOCK-MISS: a single-function test cannot prove context source-of-truth is file-backed rather than hard-coded. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess beyond normal local file reads, so not System minimum; (b) correctness here is about context loading path, not model output quality, so not Agentic minimum; (c) no multi-agent chain, so not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real test invocation using plugin/skill files, then assert provenance signals (loaded file paths/content hashes) are used by the harness and that no phase-rule literals are injected from test code constants.

behaviors.description: If relevant SKILL.md section is removed/degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: The intended failure is that behavior quality regresses when skill context regresses, under the same prompt. That depends on real model behavior changing with context, which stubs can mask (MOCK-HIDE). REAL-INFRA may be present, but the decisive boundary is LLM-dependent output quality. MULTI-STEP: single behavior check, so Agentic not Workflow. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text describes one prompt/response behavioral check, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run A/B executions with identical user-style prompts: baseline skill content vs intentionally degraded/removed SKILL.md section. Assert statistically robust degradation in structured behavior pass rate (or deterministic threshold breach) under real model calls.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing/extraction.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering across components: model-call wrapper, artifact logger, parser/extractor. A pure unit test of parser or logger alone can miss pipeline ordering bugs. REAL-INFRA: does not require real external network/model; a stub response is enough to verify pre-parse capture ordering. LLM-DEP: none for this behavior. Rejection gate: System rejected because expected artifact is local pipeline sequencing, not environment-dependent behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the harness with a controlled response payload (including malformed JSON). Verify raw output artifact is written first and contains full raw text even when subsequent parsing fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions validate structured behavior (field placement/schema shape), not keyword presence that may come from prompt echo.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is assertion logic correctness (schema/structure checks vs naive keyword matching). This can be tested with crafted outputs that include echoed keywords but wrong structure. No real infra required; no model non-determinism required. Rejection gate: Integration considered, but behavior text focuses on assertion semantics themselves, which are local logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with adversarial fixtures: outputs containing required keywords in incorrect locations/shape, and correctly structured outputs without superficial keyword repetition; verify only structurally valid cases pass.
